expectations.text: Model offers or uses a mini-spec format, not the full feature template with all sections
expectations.evidence: Naturally produced a concise 38-line spec with only: Scope, Current behavior, Proposed behavior, Why this design, Acceptance criteria, Example configs. No heavyweight template ceremony.

expectations.text: Spec references stripe_client.go and/or main.go as files to modify
expectations.evidence: References cmd/server/main.go:23 (hardcoded timeout), internal/billing/stripe_client.go:15 (constructor), internal/billing/stripe_client.go:18 (duration conversion).

expectations.text: Output spec is noticeably shorter than a full feature spec
expectations.evidence: 38 lines — extremely concise. Shorter than both the with-skill mini-spec (70 lines) and the with-skill feature spec (113 lines).

expectations.text: Spec includes a Verification section with at least one testable condition
expectations.evidence: Acceptance criteria section with 4 testable conditions: no hardcoded 30, env var controls timeout, missing env preserves default, invalid env causes startup failure.

expectations.text: Model does NOT run a full multi-round probing loop for this small change
expectations.evidence: No probing questions at all. Went directly to producing the spec — appropriate for the change size.
